title: Stand up a fresh context substrate for the port via curated carry-forward
context: The operation-framework port needs a substrate baseline; the active .context is the pi-context dev substrate and not a clean one. Substrate switching is non-destructive — the active pointer flips like a git branch and the parked substrate stays on disk (as .project / .context-jit-spec-v2 / .project-archived already coexist).
decision: Stand up a wholly new context substrate and switch the active pointer to it (non-destructively; .context stays parked). Build the operation-framework port on the new substrate, transposing the operation system except the bifurcated context (which lives in .context blocks). Carry forward only verified-live items — a curated carry-forward, not a blind greenfield and not a wholesale copy.
consequences: The port's PM state is modeled on a clean substrate, decoupled from the pi-context dev arc.
The existing .context is preserved (parked, queryable) until explicitly archived; no data loss.
The port's feature/task decomposition (PHASE-PORT-OPS) is filed on the new substrate, not the current one — so it is not filed here pending this decision's execution.
A curation triage is required: enumerate the live items and re-file only the verified-good keepers; the rest stay in the parked substrate.
